rawText:
When I think about the rise of AI, I'm reminded by the rise of literacy. A few hundred years ago, many people in society thought that maybe not everyone needed to be able to read and write. Back then, many people were tending fields or herding sheep, so maybe there was less need for written communication. And all that was needed was for the high priests and priestesses and monks to be able to read the Holy Book, and the rest of us could just go to the temple or church or the holy building and sit and listen to the high priest and priestesses read to us. Fortunately, it was since figured out that we can build a much richer society if lots of people can read and write.

Today, AI is in the hands of the high priests and priestesses. These are the highly skilled AI engineers, many of whom work in the big tech companies. And most people have access only to the AI that they build for them. I think that we can build a much richer society if we can enable everyone to help to write the future.

But why is AI largely concentrated in the big tech companies? Because many of these AI projects have been expensive to build. They may require dozens of highly skilled engineers, and they may cost millions or tens of millions of dollars to build an AI system. And the large tech companies, particularly the ones with hundreds of millions or even billions of users, have been better than anyone else at making these investments pay off because, for them, a one-size-fits-all AI system, such as one that improves web search or that recommends better products for online shopping, can be applied to very large numbers of users to generate a massive amount of revenue. But this recipe for AI does not work once you go outside the tech and internet sectors to other places where, for the most part, there are hardly any projects that apply to 100 million people or that generate comparable economics.

Let me illustrate an example. Many weekends, I drive a few minutes from my house to a local pizza store to buy a slice of Hawaiian pizza from the gentleman that owns this pizza store. And his pizza is great, but he always has a lot of cold pizzas sitting around, and every weekend some different flavor of pizza is out of stock.

AI systems are good at spotting patterns when given access to the right data, and perhaps an AI system could spot if Mediterranean pizzas sell really well on a Friday night, maybe it could suggest to him to make more of it on a Friday afternoon.

I know that there is a lot of hype about AI's need for massive data sets, and having more data does help. But contrary to the hype, AI can often work just fine even on modest amounts of data, such as the data generated by a single pizza store. So the real problem is not that there isn't enough data from the pizza store. The real problem is that the small pizza store could never serve enough customers to justify the cost of hiring an AI team.

overview.title: How AI Could Empower Any Business

sentences.original: When I think about the rise of AI, I'm reminded by the rise of literacy.
sentences.translation: AI의 부상에 대해 생각할 때, 저는 문해력의 부상이 떠오릅니다.

grammarElements.text: When I think about the rise of AI
grammarElements.label: 시간 부사절 (when)

vocabulary.example: The rise of social media changed how we communicate.
Improving literacy rates is a key goal of education.
This song reminds me of my childhood.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 다른 것을 연상시킬 때 사용
어떤 현상이나 트렌드의 성장을 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: When I think about [topic A], I'm reminded of [topic B]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 주제를 병렬로 연결하여 비유/유사성을 도입하는 패턴. 연설 오프닝으로 효과적.
examples: When I think about climate change, I'm reminded of the industrial revolution.
When I think about remote work, I'm reminded of the early days of the internet.

drillQuestions.question: When I think about modern education, I _____ reminded of ancient Greek academies.

sentences.notes: 연설의 첫 문장으로, AI와 문해력이라는 두 개념을 병치시키는 비유를 설정한다. when 종속절로 시작하여 주제를 자연스럽게 도입.

koreanAlignment.en: When I think about the rise of AI

pronunciationNotes: AI: /eɪ aɪ/ — 각 글자를 따로 발음, "에이 아이"
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역사적 유비(historical analogy) — AI의 현재를 문해력의 과거와 비교하는 프레임 설정

sentences.original: A few hundred years ago, many people in society thought that maybe not everyone needed to be able to read and write.
sentences.translation: 수백 년 전, 사회의 많은 사람들은 아마도 모든 사람이 읽고 쓸 수 있을 필요는 없다고 생각했습니다.

grammarElements.text: that maybe not everyone needed to be able to read and write
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절)

vocabulary.example: Technology has transformed modern society.
Not everyone agrees with the policy.

expressions.usage: 대략적인 과거 시간을 나타낼 때
필요성과 능력을 결합한 표현

patterns.explanation: 과거의 통념이나 믿음을 소개하는 패턴. 현재와 대비시키기 위한 역사적 맥락 설정.
examples: Fifty years ago, many people thought that computers would never be useful at home.

drillQuestions.question: A century ago, many people thought _____ space travel was impossible.

sentences.notes: "not everyone needed to"에서 부분 부정에 주목. "모든 사람이 ~할 필요는 없다"는 의미로, "nobody needed to"(아무도 필요 없다)와 다르다.

koreanAlignment.en: to be able to read and write

sentences.original: Back then, many people were tending fields or herding sheep, so maybe there was less need for written communication.
sentences.translation: 그 당시, 많은 사람들은 밭을 돌보거나 양을 몰고 있었기 때문에, 아마도 문서 의사소통에 대한 필요가 더 적었을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: so maybe there was less need for written communication
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (so)

vocabulary.example: The farmer tends his crops every day.
The shepherd herded the sheep down the hill.
Good communication is essential in any team.

expressions.usage: 과거의 특정 시기를 가리킬 때

patterns.explanation: 과거 진행형으로 당시 상황을 묘사하고 so로 그 결과를 이끌어내는 패턴.
examples: People were working long hours or farming, so there was little time for education.

drillQuestions.question: Many workers were building roads _____ digging canals, so the economy was growing.

sentences.notes: so는 결과를 나타내는 등위접속사. "tending fields or herding sheep"는 과거 사회의 일상을 묘사하여 문해력의 불필요성을 설명.

koreanAlignment.en: so maybe there was less need

sentences.original: And all that was needed was for the high priests and priestesses and monks to be able to read the Holy Book, and the rest of us could just go to the temple or church or the holy building and sit and listen to the high priest and priestesses read to us.
sentences.translation: 그리고 필요한 전부는 대제사장, 여사제, 수도사들이 성경을 읽을 수 있으면 되었고, 나머지 우리는 그저 사원이나 교회나 성스러운 건물에 가서 앉아 대제사장과 여사제들이 읽어주는 것을 들으면 됐습니다.

grammarElements.text: for the high priests and priestesses and monks to be able to read the Holy Book
grammarElements.label: 보어 (for + to부정사 구문)

grammarElements.text: and the rest of us could just go ... and sit and listen
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The priest read from the scripture.
The priestess led the ceremony.
The monks lived in a monastery on the mountain.

expressions.usage: 필요한 것이 매우 제한적임을 강조할 때
소수를 제외한 대다수를 가리킬 때

patterns.pattern: All that was needed was for [someone] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: "필요한 전부는 ~가 ~하는 것이었다"는 제한적 필요성 강조 패턴.
examples: All that was needed was for one person to speak up.
All that was needed was for the team to stay focused.

drillQuestions.question: All that was _____ was for someone to take the lead.

sentences.notes: 문해력의 독점을 묘사하는 긴 문장. "All that was needed"는 관계대명사 that이 포함된 주어부. AI가 소수 전문가에게 집중된 현재 상황과의 병렬 구조를 만들기 위한 설정.

koreanAlignment.en: was for the high priests and priestesses and monks
to be able to read the Holy Book
could just go to the temple or church

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 확장된 비유(extended analogy) — 중세 성직자 = 현대 AI 엔지니어의 병렬 구조

sentences.original: Fortunately, it was since figured out that we can build a much richer society if lots of people can read and write.
sentences.translation: 다행히도, 많은 사람들이 읽고 쓸 수 있으면 훨씬 더 풍요로운 사회를 건설할 수 있다는 것이 이후에 밝혀졌습니다.

grammarElements.text: that we can build a much richer society
grammarElements.label: 진주어 (that절)

grammarElements.text: if lots of people can read and write
grammarElements.label: 조건 부사절 (if)

vocabulary.example: Fortunately, no one was hurt.
We finally figured out the problem.

expressions.usage: 사회적으로 깨닫게 된 진실을 표현할 때. 가주어 it + 진주어 that절.

patterns.pattern: It was figured out that [fact] if [condition]
patterns.explanation: 가주어 it 구문 + 조건절. "~라면 ~라는 것이 밝혀졌다."
examples: It was figured out that students learn better if they are engaged.

drillQuestions.question: _____ was discovered that the virus spreads faster if people gather indoors.

sentences.notes: 가주어 it + 진주어 that절 + if 조건절의 복합 구조. "since"는 "이후에"라는 의미. 문해력의 민주화가 가져온 풍요를 AI에 적용하려는 논증의 전환점.

koreanAlignment.en: that we can build a much richer society
koreanAlignment.ko: 훨씬 더 풍요로운 사회를 건설할 수 있다는 것이

koreanAlignment.en: if lots of people can read and write
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 사람들이 읽고 쓸 수 있으면

sentences.original: Today, AI is in the hands of the high priests and priestesses.
sentences.translation: 오늘날 AI는 대제사장과 여사제들의 손에 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: in the hands of the high priests and priestesses
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (장소/소유)

expressions.usage: 무언가가 특정인이나 집단의 통제 하에 있을 때

patterns.explanation: "~는 ~의 손에 있다." 통제권이나 권한이 특정 집단에 집중되어 있음을 나타내는 패턴.
examples: The future is in the hands of the next generation.
The decision is in the hands of the board.

drillQuestions.question: Power is _____ the hands of a few wealthy individuals.

sentences.notes: 비유의 전환점. 문해력 비유에서 AI 현실로 전환. "high priests and priestesses"는 이전 문단의 비유를 직접 적용한 것.

koreanAlignment.en: AI is in the hands of

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 비유 연결(analogy bridge) — 역사적 비유를 현재 상황에 직접 적용

sentences.original: These are the highly skilled AI engineers, many of whom work in the big tech companies.
sentences.translation: 이들은 고도로 숙련된 AI 엔지니어들이며, 그들 중 많은 수가 대형 기술 기업에서 일합니다.

grammarElements.text: many of whom work in the big tech companies
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (계속적 용법, whom)

vocabulary.example: The team consists of highly skilled professionals.

expressions.usage: 관계대명사의 계속적 용법. 앞에 쉼표가 오며 선행사에 대한 추가 정보를 제공.

patterns.explanation: 관계대명사 whom의 계속적 용법(non-restrictive). 선행사에 대한 부가 정보를 제공한다.
examples: The students, many of whom were international, enjoyed the festival.
The employees, many of whom had worked there for decades, protested the changes.

drillQuestions.question: The scientists, many of _____ were Nobel laureates, gathered for the conference.

sentences.notes: Week 3 핵심: 관계대명사 whom의 계속적 용법. "many of whom"은 "전치사 + 관계대명사" 구조의 대표적 예. 제한적 용법(쉼표 없음)과 계속적 용법(쉼표 있음)의 차이를 이해하는 것이 중요.

koreanAlignment.en: work in the big tech companies

sentences.original: I think that we can build a much richer society if we can enable everyone to help to write the future.
sentences.translation: 저는 모든 사람이 미래를 쓰는 데 참여할 수 있게 한다면 훨씬 더 풍요로운 사회를 건설할 수 있다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: that we can build a much richer society
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절)

grammarElements.text: if we can enable everyone to help to write the future
grammarElements.label: 조건 부사절 (if)

vocabulary.example: Technology enables us to work from anywhere.
Diversity makes our culture richer.

expressions.usage: 미래를 능동적으로 형성한다는 의미의 비유적 표현
접근성과 민주화를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: I think that we can [verb] if we can [condition]
patterns.explanation: that절(주장) + if절(조건)의 결합. "~한다면 ~할 수 있다고 생각한다."
examples: I think that we can solve this problem if we work together.
I think that we can succeed if we stay committed.

drillQuestions.question: I think that we can improve _____ we invest in education.

sentences.notes: 연설의 핵심 주장(thesis). "write the future"는 문해력 비유의 연장선으로, 코드를 쓰는 것과 미래를 만드는 것을 동시에 의미하는 이중 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: we can build a much richer society
koreanAlignment.ko: 훨씬 더 풍요로운 사회를 건설할 수 있다고

koreanAlignment.en: to help to write the future

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 호소(appeal) — 민주화와 포용이라는 가치에 호소하는 핵심 메시지

sentences.original: But why is AI largely concentrated in the big tech companies?
sentences.translation: 하지만 왜 AI는 주로 대형 기술 기업에 집중되어 있을까요?

vocabulary.example: The success was largely due to teamwork.
Power is concentrated in the hands of a few.

patterns.explanation: 수사적 질문으로 청중의 관심을 끌고 다음 설명을 유도하는 패턴.
examples: But why is education still so expensive?
But why is progress so slow?

drillQuestions.question: But _____ is innovation concentrated in just a few cities?

sentences.notes: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question)으로 새로운 논점을 도입. 청중이 함께 생각하도록 유도하는 연설 기법.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 수사적 질문(rhetorical question) — 답을 아는 질문으로 논점 전환

sentences.original: Because many of these AI projects have been expensive to build.
sentences.translation: 왜냐하면 이러한 AI 프로젝트 중 많은 것들이 구축하는 데 비용이 많이 들었기 때문입니다.

vocabulary.example: AI research can be very expensive.

expressions.usage: "형용사 + to부정사" 구조로 어려움이나 비용을 표현

patterns.explanation: because로 시작하는 이유절. 현재완료와 "형용사 + to부정사"를 결합.
examples: Because these products have been difficult to manufacture, costs remain high.

drillQuestions.question: _____ the technology has been expensive to develop, only large companies can afford it.

sentences.notes: 앞의 수사적 질문에 대한 직접적 답변. Because로 시작하는 문장은 보통 비문이지만, 직전 질문의 답으로 사용할 때는 자연스럽다.

koreanAlignment.ko: 이러한 AI 프로젝트 중 많은 것들이
구축하는 데 비용이 많이 들었기 때문입니다

sentences.original: And the large tech companies, particularly the ones with hundreds of millions or even billions of users, have been better than anyone else at making these investments pay off because, for them, a one-size-fits-all AI system, such as one that improves web search or that recommends better products for online shopping, can be applied to very large numbers of users to generate a massive amount of revenue.
sentences.translation: 그리고 대형 기술 기업들, 특히 수억 명 또는 수십억 명의 사용자를 보유한 기업들은, 이러한 투자를 수익으로 전환하는 데 누구보다 뛰어났는데, 왜냐하면 그들에게는 웹 검색을 개선하거나 온라인 쇼핑을 위해 더 나은 제품을 추천하는 것과 같은 만능형 AI 시스템을 매우 많은 수의 사용자에게 적용하여 막대한 양의 수익을 창출할 수 있기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: particularly the ones with hundreds of millions or even billions of users
grammarElements.label: 삽입 수식구

grammarElements.text: have been better than anyone else
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료 비교급)

grammarElements.text: at making these investments pay off
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (분야)

grammarElements.text: because, for them, a one-size-fits-all AI system ... can be applied to ...
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (because)

grammarElements.text: such as one that improves web search or that recommends better products
grammarElements.label: 예시 삽입구 (such as)

vocabulary.example: The company made a significant investment in AI.
Hard work eventually pays off.
The company reported record revenue this quarter.
There is no one-size-fits-all solution to this problem.

expressions.usage: 특정 분야에서의 최고 역량을 강조할 때
구체적인 예시를 제시할 때
큰 규모를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] have been better than [comparison] at [verb]-ing because [reason]
patterns.explanation: 현재완료 비교급 + at동명사 + because 이유절의 결합. 특정 분야의 우위와 그 원인을 설명.
examples: Start-ups have been better than corporations at adapting because they are more agile.

drillQuestions.question: Tech firms have been better than traditional businesses _____ adopting new tools.

sentences.notes: 매우 긴 복합문. 주절 + 삽입 수식구(particularly...) + because 이유절 + such as 예시구 + 관계대명사 that절이 중첩. 영어 학술/비즈니스 텍스트에서 흔히 볼 수 있는 정보 밀도 높은 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: particularly the ones with hundreds of millions or even billions of users
koreanAlignment.ko: 특히 수억 명 또는 수십억 명의 사용자를 보유한 기업들은

koreanAlignment.en: have been better than anyone else
at making these investments pay off
can be applied to very large numbers of users
to generate a massive amount of revenue
koreanAlignment.ko: 막대한 양의 수익을 창출할 수 있기 때문입니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 논리적 전개(logical exposition) — 원인과 결과를 상세히 설명하는 분석적 서술

sentences.original: But this recipe for AI does not work once you go outside the tech and internet sectors to other places where, for the most part, there are hardly any projects that apply to 100 million people or that generate comparable economics.
sentences.translation: 하지만 이 AI 처방은 기술 및 인터넷 분야를 벗어나 대부분의 경우 1억 명에게 적용되거나 비슷한 경제성을 창출하는 프로젝트가 거의 없는 다른 곳으로 가면 작동하지 않습니다.

grammarElements.text: once you go outside the tech and internet sectors
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건 부사절 (once)

grammarElements.text: to other places where ... there are hardly any projects
grammarElements.label: 관계부사절 (where)

vocabulary.example: There is no simple recipe for success.
The technology sector is growing rapidly.
The two systems are not comparable.
There is hardly any time left.

expressions.usage: 비유적으로 성공이나 실패의 공식을 나타낼 때
일반적인 상황을 설명할 때의 한정 표현

patterns.pattern: [This approach] does not work once [condition] where [limitation]
patterns.explanation: once 조건절 + where 관계부사절로 한계 상황을 설명하는 패턴.
examples: This strategy does not work once you enter markets where consumer behavior is different.

drillQuestions.question: The method does not work _____ you deal with small data sets.

sentences.notes: 핵심 논점의 전환: 대기업 중심 AI의 한계를 제시. once(일단 ~하면), where(관계부사), that(관계대명사) 등 여러 종속절이 중첩된 고급 복합문.

koreanAlignment.en: once you go outside the tech and internet sectors

vocabulary.example: Let me illustrate this with a diagram.

expressions.usage: 설명이나 예시를 제시하기 전에 사용하는 도입 표현

patterns.explanation: "제가 ~하겠습니다"라는 정중한 제안/도입 표현. 프레젠테이션에서 자주 사용.
examples: Let me explain the process.
Let me show you what I mean.

sentences.notes: 전형적인 프레젠테이션 전환 표현. 추상적 논의에서 구체적 예시로 넘어갈 때 사용.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환 신호(transition signal) — 추상에서 구체로의 전환을 알림

sentences.original: And his pizza is great, but he always has a lot of cold pizzas sitting around, and every weekend some different flavor of pizza is out of stock.
sentences.translation: 그리고 그의 피자는 훌륭하지만, 항상 차가운 피자가 많이 남아 있고, 매주 주말마다 다른 맛의 피자가 품절됩니다.

grammarElements.text: he always has a lot of cold pizzas sitting around
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (5형식: have + 목적어 + 현재분사)

grammarElements.text: and every weekend some different flavor of pizza is out of stock
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The product is temporarily out of stock.
What flavor of ice cream do you want?

expressions.usage: 물건이 사용되지 않고 방치되어 있을 때
상품의 재고가 없을 때

patterns.explanation: 긍정적 요소를 인정한 후 but과 and로 문제점들을 나열하는 패턴.
examples: The app is great, but it crashes often, and the updates are slow.

drillQuestions.question: The restaurant is popular, _____ the wait times are long.

sentences.notes: "has a lot of cold pizzas sitting around"에서 have + 목적어 + 현재분사는 5형식 구문. "피자가 남아있는 상태를 가지고 있다"는 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: but he always has a lot of cold pizzas sitting around
koreanAlignment.ko: 항상 차가운 피자가 많이 남아 있고

koreanAlignment.en: and every weekend some different flavor of pizza

sentences.original: AI systems are good at spotting patterns when given access to the right data, and perhaps an AI system could spot if Mediterranean pizzas sell really well on a Friday night, maybe it could suggest to him to make more of it on a Friday afternoon.
sentences.translation: AI 시스템은 적절한 데이터에 접근할 수 있을 때 패턴을 발견하는 데 뛰어나며, 아마도 AI 시스템이 지중해식 피자가 금요일 밤에 정말 잘 팔리는지 감지할 수 있다면, 금요일 오후에 더 많이 만들라고 그에게 제안할 수 있을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: when given access to the right data
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건 부사절 (when + 분사구문)

grammarElements.text: and perhaps an AI system could spot if Mediterranean pizzas sell really well
grammarElements.label: 등위절 + 조건절 (if)

grammarElements.text: maybe it could suggest to him to make more of it
grammarElements.label: 결과절

vocabulary.example: Can you spot the difference between these two pictures?
The AI spotted a pattern in the sales data.
I suggest we take a different approach.

expressions.usage: 능력이나 장점을 표현할 때
조건부 상황을 설명할 때. 분사구문의 수동형.

patterns.explanation: "~는 ~할 때 ~을 잘한다"는 능력과 조건을 결합하는 패턴.
examples: Humans are good at making decisions when given enough information.

drillQuestions.question: AI is good _____ spotting patterns in large data sets.

sentences.notes: "when given"은 "when (they are) given"의 축약형으로 분사구문의 수동형. 피자 가게 예시를 통해 AI의 실용적 가치를 구체적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: AI systems are good at spotting patterns
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 시스템은 패턴을 발견하는 데 뛰어나며

koreanAlignment.en: when given access to the right data
koreanAlignment.ko: 적절한 데이터에 접근할 수 있을 때

koreanAlignment.en: and perhaps an AI system could spot
koreanAlignment.ko: 아마도 AI 시스템이 감지할 수 있다면

koreanAlignment.en: if Mediterranean pizzas sell really well on a Friday night
koreanAlignment.ko: 지중해식 피자가 금요일 밤에 정말 잘 팔리는지

koreanAlignment.en: maybe it could suggest to him
to make more of it on a Friday afternoon

sentences.original: I know that there is a lot of hype about AI's need for massive data sets, and having more data does help.
sentences.translation: 저는 대규모 데이터 세트에 대한 AI의 필요성에 대해 많은 과대광고가 있다는 것을 알고, 더 많은 데이터를 갖는 것이 도움이 되는 것은 사실입니다.

grammarElements.text: that there is a lot of hype about AI's need for massive data sets
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절)

grammarElements.text: and having more data does help
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (강조의 does)

vocabulary.example: There is a lot of hype around this new product.
The project requires massive amounts of data.
Researchers analyzed a large data set.

expressions.usage: 어떤 주제가 과장되게 다뤄질 때
강조의 do 용법. 일반동사 앞에 do/does/did를 넣어 강조.

patterns.explanation: 인정 + 강조의 do: "~라는 것을 알고, ~하는 것이 실제로 ~하긴 한다."
examples: I know that there are risks, and planning ahead does reduce them.

drillQuestions.question: Practice _____ make perfect, even if it takes time.

sentences.notes: "does help"에서 does는 강조의 조동사. "도움이 되긴 된다"는 양보의 의미. 통념을 일부 인정한 후 반박하는 논증 기법의 시작.

koreanAlignment.en: there is a lot of hype about AI's need
koreanAlignment.ko: AI의 필요성에 대해 많은 과대광고가 있다는 것을

koreanAlignment.en: and having more data does help
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 더 많은 데이터를 갖는 것이 도움이 되는 것은 사실입니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보 후 반박(concession-rebuttal) — 상대 주장을 인정한 후 반박을 준비

sentences.original: But contrary to the hype, AI can often work just fine even on modest amounts of data, such as the data generated by a single pizza store.
sentences.translation: 하지만 과대광고와는 반대로, AI는 종종 단일 피자 가게가 생성하는 데이터와 같은 적당한 양의 데이터에서도 충분히 잘 작동할 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: even on modest amounts of data
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (양보)

grammarElements.text: such as the data generated by a single pizza store
grammarElements.label: 예시구 (such as)

vocabulary.example: Contrary to popular belief, bats are not blind.
The project achieved good results with a modest budget.

expressions.usage: 일반적 믿음이나 기대와 반대되는 사실을 제시할 때
기대에 부합하거나 그 이상으로 잘 기능할 때

patterns.pattern: Contrary to [belief/claim], [subject] can [verb] even on [limited resource]
patterns.explanation: "~와는 달리, ~도 ~할 수 있다." 통념을 뒤집는 반박 패턴.
examples: Contrary to popular belief, small companies can innovate even on limited budgets.

drillQuestions.question: _____ to expectations, the project succeeded with minimal funding.

sentences.notes: "contrary to"는 반박의 핵심 표현. 통념(AI에는 빅데이터가 필수)을 뒤집어 소규모 데이터의 가능성을 주장하는 연설의 핵심 논점.

koreanAlignment.en: AI can often work just fine
koreanAlignment.ko: AI는 종종 충분히 잘 작동할 수 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: even on modest amounts of data
such as the data generated by a single pizza store
koreanAlignment.ko: 단일 피자 가게가 생성하는 데이터와 같은

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 통념 반박(myth-busting) — 널리 퍼진 오해를 직접적으로 반박

sentences.original: So the real problem is not that there isn't enough data from the pizza store.
sentences.translation: 따라서 진짜 문제는 피자 가게의 데이터가 충분하지 않다는 것이 아닙니다.

grammarElements.text: not that there isn't enough data from the pizza store
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부정 + that절)

expressions.phrase: the real problem is not that
expressions.usage: 흔히 오해되는 문제의 본질을 바로잡을 때

patterns.pattern: The real problem is not that [X]. The real problem is that [Y].
patterns.explanation: 문제의 핵심을 재정의하는 반복 대조 패턴. "진짜 문제는 X가 아니라 Y다."
examples: The real problem is not that we lack talent. The real problem is that we lack opportunity.

drillQuestions.question: The real problem is _____ that prices are high, but that wages are low.

sentences.notes: "not that ... the real problem is that ..."의 반복 대조 구조. 다음 문장과 함께 읽으면 문제를 재정의하는 수사 기법이 드러남.

koreanAlignment.en: is not that there isn't enough data

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 재정의(reframing) — 문제의 핵심을 재설정하여 새로운 관점을 제시

sentences.original: The real problem is that the small pizza store could never serve enough customers to justify the cost of hiring an AI team.
sentences.translation: 진짜 문제는 작은 피자 가게가 AI 팀을 고용하는 비용을 정당화할 만큼 충분한 고객을 절대로 확보할 수 없다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: that the small pizza store could never serve enough customers
grammarElements.label: 보어절 (that절)

grammarElements.text: to justify the cost of hiring an AI team
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: The results don't justify the expense.
We need to hire more engineers.

expressions.usage: 불가능성을 강하게 표현할 때
비용 대비 효과를 평가할 때 사용하는 비즈니스 표현

patterns.pattern: The real problem is that [subject] could never [verb] enough to justify [cost]
patterns.explanation: "진짜 문제는 ~가 비용을 정당화할 만큼 ~할 수 없다는 것이다." 경제적 한계를 설명하는 패턴.
examples: The real problem is that a single farm could never produce enough to justify the cost of automation.

drillQuestions.question: The small shop cannot sell enough products to _____ hiring extra staff.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장과 대조를 이루며 진짜 문제를 정의. 경제성(economics)이 AI 접근성의 핵심 장벽임을 지적하는 연설의 핵심 논점.

koreanAlignment.en: the small pizza store could never serve enough customers
koreanAlignment.ko: 작은 피자 가게가 충분한 고객을 절대로 확보할 수 없다는

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 핵심 논점 제시(key argument) — 경제적 장벽이라는 문제의 본질을 밝힘
